summary_one_line: A multi-glyph USE syllable whose leading glyph carries use_category USE(R) (Repha), with end-start>1, so the reorder true side fires.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A buffer of repeated fixed-stride glyph records (offset 0x00=0x64, 0x01=0xa8, then a 02 1f 01 00 tail) that yields a syllable of length >1 whose first glyph maps to use_category USE(R); the leading-record byte and multiplicity must both hold simultaneously.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Naive winner seeds 2-5 are built from the repeated record skeleton (e.g. seed 2 '64 a8 00 05 02 1f 01 00 63 a8 00 00 02 1f 01 00'), and the BYTE DIFF shows W concentrates 64(d)x4 at 0x00 and a8(.)x4 at 0x01, so havoc byte-add/inc/delete on this self-similar layout reliably produces >1 USE(R)-led glyphs.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Cmplog loser seeds carry CMP/token-derived literal runs (e.g. 'true', ';;;;;sn', 'art', repeated 'b8'/'3b' fills) rather than the 64 a8 record stride, so they do not present a USE(R) leading glyph in a length>1 syllable and the true side never fires (loser resolved=0/10, blocked=10).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: I2SRandReplace HURTS here: cmplog spends mutation energy substituting CmpLogObserver operands into the input, dragging it toward literal CMP targets and away from the self-similar repeated-record layout that the structural USE(R) gate needs; naive, lacking I2S, keeps doing plain havoc on the record skeleton and wins. The enabling technique for the winner is havoc-only mutation.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The same multi-glyph syllable with a USE(R)-category leading glyph (length>1), reached when intermediate equality/category comparisons on the glyph-category path are partially satisfied.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets on top of cmplog, so partial progress toward info[start].use_category()==USE(R) is rewarded as new coverage; the winner resolves 9/10 with blocked duration only 5.60h vs the loser's 23.60h, indicating the gradient feedback steers it onto the USE(R) record faster.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Cmplog has the identical I2S mutator and the same edge-bucket feedback but NO CMP_MAP gradient bucket, so it gets no partial-credit signal toward the equality gate and stalls (resolved=0/10, blocked=10, dur 23.60h); the only technique difference between the two arms is value_profile.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: CMP_MAP gradient: the value_profile feedback distinguishing vpc from cmplog records Hamming/prefix proximity of the use_category comparison operands, rewarding inputs that get closer to USE(R), which guides corpus growth onto the gate that plain cmplog cannot sense.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked true side requires the start glyph's use_category to equal USE(R) and the syllable to be longer than one glyph.
cmplog is the loser in both decisive pairs, resolving 0/10 and blocked 10/10.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: cmplog                           0       10          0  loser (I2S vs naive); loser (value_profile vs value_profile_cmplog)

evidence_trail.claim: Pair 1 is naive>cmplog with the differing technique being I2S.
Naive winner seed 2 is built from a repeated fixed-stride record skeleton beginning 64 a8 00.
The byte diff shows winner inputs concentrate 0x64 at offset 0 and 0xa8 at offset 1 while loser inputs are scattered.
Loser cmplog seeds carry CMP/token literal runs rather than the record stride, e.g. an 'art' token and a long ';' fill.
cmplog's mutators add I2SRandReplace which substitutes CmpLogObserver operands into the input.
I2SRandReplace scans the input for byte sequences matching one operand and substitutes the other operand.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive uses only a havoc + token mutation stack with no I2S.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: Pair 2 is value_profile_cmplog>cmplog with the differing technique being value_profile.
value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets to cmplog's feedback.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
edge counters,
per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets.

evidence_trail.claim: The vpc winner resolves with much shorter blocked duration (5.60h) than the cmplog loser (23.60h).

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Neither hypothesis claims I2SRandReplace, so lineage dash-row verification is not mandatory. cmplog is the LOSER in both pairs (resolved=0/10): the I2S-equipped arm is hurt, not helped. Pair 1's winner (naive) is havoc-only and pair 2's winner (vpc) wins via the CMP_MAP gradient that cmplog lacks. Note multi_feature carries a distinct mechanism per hypothesis (havoc-only for pair 1, CMP_MAP gradient for pair 2); the top-level claimed_mechanism records the gradient axis for pair 2.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with the same USE(R)-equality-plus-length>1 gate where cmplog resolves it as fast as naive and value_profile_cmplog, i.e. enabling I2S/CMP_MAP shows no penalty/no gradient benefit, would refute both the I2S-anti and gradient-rescue claims.

weakest_evidence_point: Pair 1's havoc-only attribution rests on the inference that I2S actively diverts cmplog toward CMP literals; it is also possible cmplog simply never sampled the record layout by chance rather than being dragged off it.
